124455.explanation: A local shop, such as those selling cheese or bulbs, makes our Teylingen so distinctive and we must cherish that. However, we must ensure that no (large) campsites are built on our open bulb fields.

124456.explanation: The generation of sustainable energy is important for the energy transition in Teylingen, which is why the VVD also supports the sustainability loan. However, financial support does not always have to come directly from the municipality.

124458.explanation: Teylingen made an offer to the COA (due to legal obligations) to accommodate a limited number of minor refugees, but the COA has withdrawn from this. For the VVD, it is not necessary to make further plans now.

124463.explanation: Having our own organization can be costly. However, initiatives from our villages, such as associations like the VWF with the fireworks show during Kaagweek, can be facilitated.

124468.explanation: Teylingen already has a fund for this purpose, and the VVD supports continuing this fund to help people.

124475.explanation: The municipality must prioritize safety in public spaces. For this, additional enforcement officers (boa) capacity is needed. Reports must be addressed more quickly and nuisance must be reduced.

124476.explanation: Especially in nuisance areas such as stations, to ensure your bike is still there when you return by train.

124482.explanation: Guidance and active support in finding suitable work is important. If social work can contribute to this, it is a possibility.

124483.explanation: In Teylingen, we are there for each other in difficult times.

124484.explanation: For help, you can turn to the municipality. That help can consist of much more than raising the poverty line; people can benefit more from other measures.

124487.explanation: Lowering the maximum speed should not be an end in itself. The goal is to travel safely from A to B in Teylingen, which can be done in some places within built-up areas (such as Oosthoutlaan in Voorhout) at 50 km/h.

124489.explanation: The quality of youth care and limiting waiting times are paramount. Additionally, costs must be critically reviewed.

124490.explanation: As much as we would like to, it is not realistic to lower the property tax (ozb). This is due to rising costs and reduced income from the national government. However, we will always critically review expenses to keep Teylingen affordable.

124493.explanation: We have limited space in Teylingen, so more industrial sites are not necessarily needed. However, the VVD stands for safe and well-maintained industrial sites that Teylingen already has.

124495.explanation: The municipality is currently fulfilling a legal obligation to house status holders. As long as this obligation exists, it is a responsibility to continue doing so.

124498.explanation: If a family is unable to pay for swimming lessons, they can approach the municipality for support. There is a fund for this purpose. The VVD supports maintaining that fund.

124501.explanation: Only if it fits within our villages.

124503.explanation: The VVD supports maintaining the current number of parking spaces. This allows for a good balance between parking on one hand and pleasant shopping streets to stay in on the other.

124504.explanation: Traffic safety is important, while shops in the center must remain easily accessible for customers.

124505.explanation: In places where permanent construction is not immediately possible, temporary housing (which can be temporary for up to 30 years!) can definitely be a solution. The VVD advocates for a location with 100% affordable homes.

124506.explanation: The VVD sees opportunities for geothermal energy and innovative local solutions, provided they are affordable and in cooperation with the region. If in the coming years this proves unaffordable or cannot be done regionally, other solutions may be considered.

124507.explanation: New construction at the current location can provide modern waste processing with reduced nuisance for local residents.

124509.explanation: The VVD stands for preserving our characteristic bulb region. However, small-scale, truly customized solutions can sometimes be considered, for example for small, uneconomical plots of bulb fields.

124455.explanation: The CDA is positive about such initiatives, within municipal frameworks.

124456.explanation: The municipality should support initiatives for energy generation. Financial support depends on the available budgets.

124458.explanation: As long as the dispersal law exists, the municipality must contribute to asylum seeker reception. The CDA favors small-scale reception. So no large-scale AZC. The safety of the surroundings must be guaranteed. An AZC is a large-scale reception center for 300 to 1500 refugees.

124468.explanation: There are arrangements for people who cannot afford their membership fees.

124470.explanation: Maintenance of schools is the responsibility of school boards. The municipality is responsible for new construction or major renovations.

124475.explanation: The number of enforcement officers (boa’s) is limited in our municipality. Because unsafe situations occur more often, additional officers can monitor these.

124476.explanation: Many bicycles are stolen at the stations in Sassenheim and Voorhout. Extra surveillance, for example by cameras, can be a good and effective solution.

124483.explanation: There are arrangements for people who cannot pay their municipal taxes. That must remain so.

124489.explanation: Youth care must first and foremost be effective. The quality delivered must be good. Additionally, price is important because the youth assistance budget is under great pressure.

124490.explanation: Teylingen's budget will not be structurally balanced in the coming years. This means cuts and revenue-increasing measures must be considered. The CDA wants to maintain the current level of facilities (such as the swimming pool).

124493.explanation: There are already several industrial areas in Teylingen. This is sufficient. More could harm the living environment, which the CDA values highly.

124495.explanation: It concerns equal treatment of people urgently seeking social housing.

124501.explanation: The CDA envisions a maximum height of 6 or 7 floors. The residential flats must fit well within the surroundings.

124506.explanation: It is important to carefully consider the risks of a heat network and what the costs will be for the residents (the users).

124507.explanation: Provided that the construction meets the required environmental permits.

124508.explanation: It is important that ongoing attention is given to the efficiency of the organization.

124509.explanation: The CDA wants to preserve the current area of first-class bulb-growing land.

124455.explanation: This will always depend on the location. We are careful with our rural areas and not every place is suitable.

124456.explanation: This is the ideal picture: residents jointly produce their own green and affordable electricity with a wind turbine or solar panels. They use the electricity onsite, so the grid is not burdened. Energy cooperatives are an asset to our municipality and should be encouraged.

124458.explanation: We have a duty to provide shelter for people fleeing war and violence.

124463.explanation: The best New Year's celebration is one we all share together, with one big fireworks show or three smaller ones organized by the municipality. This keeps it safe and allows us to enjoy a cozy New Year's Eve together.

124468.explanation: Sports provide a lot of energy and satisfaction. And they keep you healthy. But for people with welfare benefits, sports are often too expensive. We want to maintain sports and cultural support and explore whether it can be expanded.

124470.explanation: A good school deserves a good building. It is the municipality's responsibility to work with schools to ensure school buildings where learning is safe and pleasant. Investing in this is investing in our future.

124475.explanation: In Teylingen we have sufficient enforcement officers. We must deploy them for tasks that are problematic. Enforcement should not be done for its own sake but to solve problems, such as safety at a station, traffic/parking nuisance, or other issues.

124476.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But we do not believe more cameras are the right solution. Your privacy is a great good that should not be infringed lightly. That is why we prefer enforcement officers on the street who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

124482.explanation: Most people are not on social assistance because they don't want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra requirements and conditions actually create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

124483.explanation: If debt forgiveness of municipal taxes stops, people with the lowest incomes in our municipality will get into big trouble. They simply cannot miss that money. We believe everyone should be able to keep their head above water, which is why we support debt forgiveness.

124484.explanation: This is too general. As a municipality, we must help people living in poverty or prevent poverty. This often requires customized solutions. Increasingly, we see this is also necessary for residents with a fixed income too low to make ends meet.

124486.explanation: There must be customized solutions. If there is a shortage, what is needed must be examined. At the same time, many issues can often be solved by providing more options for safe cables over the road.

124487.explanation: The figures clearly show: a lower maximum speed makes traffic in our municipality much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with reduced mobility, this makes a huge difference. Moreover, the streets become quieter and the air cleaner.

124488.explanation: Freedom of choice is important for everyone.

124489.explanation: The waiting lists and problems in youth assistance and youth care are enormous. You don’t solve this by cutting more and always choosing the cheapest options. We must especially ensure certainty and provide help that matches the concerns of our youth.

124490.explanation: To maintain facilities and remain financially healthy at the same time, we will need to raise the property tax (ozb) next year. We do not want to choose to dismantle necessary facilities for our residents.

124493.explanation: There is a great need for business parks. We allow expansion as long as it involves high-quality business activities.

124495.explanation: Status holders can truly integrate only if they have a home. With a residence permit, people are supposed to start here with language, work, school, and care. That does not succeed from a shelter location or with temporary, uncertain addresses. Not moving on to a home means longer stays in asylum reception centers.

124498.explanation: The Netherlands is a water country and every child should learn to swim. Including children whose parents cannot afford swimming lessons. Unfortunately, this is still not fairly arranged. The best solution: school swimming for every child, with support from the municipality.

124499.explanation: Preferably not in Teylingen. In any case, based on a permit that ensures sufficient distance from vulnerable locations. Preferably not directly near schools, playgrounds, youth centers, and sports clubs.

124501.explanation: We have a huge housing shortage and action is needed. Building somewhat higher in more locations where possible can help counter the housing shortage.

124503.explanation: In the new center plans, we focus on parking as much as possible beside the centers (for example at supermarkets) within walking distance of the shops. We do not choose to create more parking spaces here. Instead, we want more space for pedestrians and cyclists.

124504.explanation: Last year, a center plan for Sassenheim was drawn up in consultation with stakeholders, which will be implemented in the coming years. It focuses on vitality, attractiveness, climate adaptability, and being car-reduced.

124505.explanation: Temporary housing is not the solution to the housing shortage but is something we must do. It helps people who are currently stuck, starters, urgent seekers, separated parents, status holders, people from shelters or care, freeing up places elsewhere.

124506.explanation: It is important that residents’ energy costs remain affordable. Large investments in a heat network should never mean higher prices for residents.

124507.explanation: The plans must first be assessed for traffic, odor, and noise nuisances. Only if this can be done according to the rules can it proceed. New construction offers a chance to better control nuisances than currently: with smarter site layout, better screening, and modern installations.

124508.explanation: Cooperation saves Teylingen costs. It is unfortunate that it is not always going smoothly. Therefore, we must continue to improve.

124509.explanation: We are careful with the rural area. That's why we mainly want to build in existing neighborhoods and cores, within built-up areas. But building on the edge of built-up areas, such as on Eikenhorstlaan in Voorhout, is unavoidable.

124455.explanation: D66 wants to give farmers space to operate and innovate. New activities are possible but not without conditions for the environment and landscape. Customized solutions and good consideration are essential.

124456.explanation: D66 supports residents who want to generate sustainable energy together. Municipal financial support helps get good initiatives off the ground. This way we accelerate the energy transition from the bottom up.

124458.explanation: D66 advocates for manageable reception and wants to fulfill the legal task for reception assigned to us by the national government. An asylum seekers' center can only operate with clear conditions for safety, scale, and livability.

124463.explanation: D66 sees a joint fireworks show as a great way to start the new year together. It offers a safe and festive alternative to private fireworks. This strengthens togetherness and increases safety on New Year’s Eve.

124468.explanation: D66 believes everyone should have the opportunity to participate and live healthily. Support for sports participation can help. Targeted support requires clear criteria and demonstrable social impact.

124470.explanation: D66 wants every child to learn in a safe, healthy, and inspiring environment. That is why the municipality invests in improving all school buildings. This gives students and teachers the best foundation to develop themselves.

124475.explanation: D66 chooses visible enforcement and a safe public space. More special enforcement officers ensure quicker action and clear boundaries. This makes Teylingen safer and keeps order on the streets.

124476.explanation: D66 is open to camera surveillance as a means to increase safety. That can only be targeted, temporary, and with strict privacy safeguards. Camera surveillance is not an end in itself but an option where truly needed, such as stations.

124482.explanation: D66 believes that participation may be expected from those receiving welfare benefits. Meaningful social work can contribute to this but is not a strict obligation. Guidance, customization, and perspective remain leading.

124483.explanation: D66 believes the municipality must keep an eye on residents with low incomes. Ending debt forgiveness increases financial stress and inequality. Through customized support, the municipality remains social and just.

124484.explanation: D66 wants social policy that is clear and sustainable. Raising income thresholds is not always the right approach. We focus support on those who need it most, with room for customization.

124486.explanation: D66 wants to further stimulate electric driving in our municipality. More charging points make switching to cleaner transport easier, but it must be demand-driven and only where appropriate. Together, we work towards sustainable and future-oriented mobility.

124487.explanation: D66 wants safe, livable, and quiet streets in our villages. A maximum speed of 30 km/h increases traffic safety and reduces nuisance. This makes public spaces more pleasant for everyone.

124488.explanation: D66 believes care should align as much as possible with who a person is. An absolute right to choice is not always feasible in practice. However, we strive for maximum room for preferences within what is achievable.

124489.explanation: D66 believes quality and continuity must lead youth care. Selecting solely based on price harms good care for children and families. The municipality must choose what works, not just what is cheap.

124490.explanation: D66 stands for a municipality that can continue to seriously perform its duties. Lowering the property tax (ozb) undermines investments in care, safety, and facilities. Popular tax cuts should not come at the expense of a strong municipality.

124493.explanation: D66 wants to handle the scarce space in Teylingen carefully. New business parks are only an option if there is demonstrable need. We choose customized solutions, quality, and better use of existing locations.

124495.explanation: Building enough homes is the only solution to the housing crisis. D66 believes housing allocation must be fair and explainable. Groups with urgency must retain that possibility.

124498.explanation: D66 believes the municipality must continue investing in swimming skills for children. This can be done through (existing) schemes that support families with low incomes to afford swimming lessons. This makes swimming safety accessible for every child, regardless of the financial situation at home.

124499.explanation: D66 is not automatically in favor of every coffeeshop: it can cause nuisance and increase pressure on enforcement. But regulation can reduce street dealing, provided there are strict conditions for location, safety, and quick response to problems. It is time for a fully legal chain, including legal cultivation.

124501.explanation: D66 sees high-rise buildings as inevitable to tackle the housing shortage. However, extremely tall residential towers do not fit the village character of Warmond, Sassenheim, and Voorhout. Build a bit higher where possible, stay low where needed.

124503.explanation: D66 rejects the idea of automatically creating more parking spaces. Parking spaces should be where they are needed. Where not needed, we consciously choose greenery, play areas, and quality of life.

124504.explanation: D66 chooses a lively and well-accessible center in Sassenheim. Therefore, we aim for a low-car center, with more space for pedestrians, cyclists, and lingering. We see a fully car-free center as an extreme and not as a starting point.

124505.explanation: D66 sees temporary housing as urgently needed to quickly relieve the housing shortage. They provide immediate space for starters, urgent seekers, and other housing applicants. Therefore, we want to enable more places for flexible and temporary construction.

124506.explanation: D66 sees a regional heat network as an opportunity for clean and affordable energy for residents. Cooperation in the Bollenstreek makes sustainable heat more efficient and cheaper. This lowers energy bills and accelerates sustainability.

124507.explanation: D66 believes the statement reasons incorrectly. The project does not necessarily have to proceed; rather, livability, safety, and the environment must be guaranteed. Only if this is well demonstrated can a renewed waste processing company be built on ’s Gravendamseweg in Voorhout.

124508.explanation: D66 sees cooperation with Lisse and Hillegom as a wise choice. Cooperation can lead to more efficient working and lower costs.

124509.explanation: D66 wants to carefully handle bulb-growing land and solve the housing shortage. Building homes on bulb land is not the first choice but can sometimes be necessary. Especially considering the enormous need for housing.

124455.explanation: Trilokaal finds it important that entrepreneurs have space to run their business. Administrative burdens must be reduced and the municipality should actively seek cooperation with all entrepreneurs, but automatic permission is not possible and every case must always be carefully reviewed.

124456.explanation: Feasible, affordable, and reliable sustainability is what Trilokaal stands for. Trilokaal welcomes citizen initiatives because we do sustainability together. The municipality can support this. The form of support will differ per initiative.

124458.explanation: Trilokaal is not in favor of establishing a permanent asylum seekers center in Teylingen due to the space it occupies. At the same time, we are open to humane shelter and support in small-scale temporary variants that do not impose extra costs on the municipality.

124463.explanation: Due to the upcoming national fireworks ban, consumer fireworks will soon be a thing of the past. As an alternative, Trilokaal favors organized fireworks shows per village, for example with municipal support. However, organizing these is not a task of the municipality.

124468.explanation: To support people with social assistance benefits, Teylingen has a scheme for sports and culture. Trilokaal finds it important to support people but does not want to pay the full membership fees. We would like to revise the amount of support, as it is often insufficient.

124470.explanation: Neat, modern, and sustainable school buildings are an important foundation for good education. In the coming years, work will be done to renovate all school buildings thanks to the Integral Housing Plan (IHP) adopted by the municipal council.

124475.explanation: For years, the capacity of BOAs (public enforcement officers) and police has been inadequate. Trilokaal therefore wants to increase the number of BOAs and police officers in Teylingen to the national standard. Better enforcement is necessary for social and traffic safety.

124476.explanation: To ensure safety, camera surveillance can be a useful tool. For example, at stations or other risk areas, even if only temporarily. This requires customization as privacy laws prevent camera surveillance from being applied everywhere without consideration.

124482.explanation: Socially meaningful work can have positive effects, such as gaining contacts and finding purpose. However, not everyone receiving benefits can work, for example due to a physical disability. This requires customized solutions. Ultimately, the municipality offers a safety net, not a hammock.

124483.explanation: There are good agreements in Teylingen about remission for people with lower incomes, and these should certainly continue.

124484.explanation: Thanks in part to Trilokaal, the income limit for poverty aid has been raised to 130%. This is a good development. Not only the income limit matters, but also someone’s circumstances in poverty, such as debts.

124486.explanation: The charging network needs expansion now that there are more electric cars, but electric driving is not affordable for every resident. A good balance in parking facilities is therefore necessary.

124487.explanation: Trilokaal finds it extremely important that streets and neighborhoods are designed to be safe. In residential areas, 30 km/h is the standard. Main roads with separate bike paths should remain 50 km/h zones. If it improves safety, Trilokaal is willing to consider lowering speeds.

124488.explanation: Trilokaal believes customized care is the key to improving healthcare. For effective care, trust and safety are important. Where possible, choosing a different care provider can help, but given the pressure on care, this is not always possible.

124489.explanation: There is increasing demand for (specialist) youth care. Trilokaal believes in customized care. We must carefully monitor youth care costs, but quality is leading to achieve good results.

124490.explanation: If taxes can be lowered, we should do so. Trilokaal opposes an increase of the property tax (ozb). In case of deficits, we first look to cut our own expenses before raising taxes. However, there comes a point where cuts would harm Teylingen’s services too much.

124493.explanation: Given Teylingen's location, it is attractive for (large) companies to settle here. Trilokaal questions whether expanding business parks is feasible given the limited space and pressure on the road network.

124495.explanation: Trilokaal wants residents of our villages to have a better starting position to live in (or return to) Teylingen. In practice, very few homes go to refugees. It is therefore important that the municipality builds sufficient social rental housing.

124498.explanation: Learning to swim is important for every child. In Teylingen, school swimming is hardly utilized, and Trilokaal sees this as a cost-saving option. However, Trilokaal finds it important that families who cannot afford swimming lessons receive financial support.

124499.explanation: A coffeeshop has a large impact on the surroundings and encourages drug use. Trilokaal sees no room for a coffeeshop in Teylingen.

124501.explanation: We prefer somewhat taller buildings and densification within the built-up area rather than losing valuable nature or polder landscapes forever. How high we build, however, depends heavily on the location; Teylingen should remain a village municipality.

124503.explanation: Centers must remain easily accessible for people who do their shopping by car. Therefore, enough parking spaces must remain available in the centers, even after reorganization. This prevents cars parking on sidewalks, which inconveniences disabled residents.

124504.explanation: A car-free center is the death knell for the village. Residents must be able to reach the center for their shopping on foot, by bike, and by car. Parts of Sassenheim’s center should be able to become more car-restricted after reorganization.

124505.explanation: Where possible, Trilokaal is a strong supporter of temporary housing forms, such as Teydelijk in Voorhout. However, space is scarce, so strict choices must be made.

124506.explanation: Trilokaal supports exploring the possibilities of establishing a district heating network in the Bollenstreek, as is currently being done. However, we remain critical of the costs and technical feasibility.

124507.explanation: The municipal council, partly thanks to various proposals from Trilokaal, has made adjustments to the framework for new construction. Whether the new construction takes place at this location or elsewhere is still undecided by Trilokaal. The involved company will need to significantly improve its application.

124508.explanation: Regional cooperation is good where it benefits Teylingen. Over recent years, Trilokaal has taken a critical stance toward the functioning of the civil service cooperation (HLTsamen). Trilokaal is also against a municipal merger with Lisse and Hillegom.

124509.explanation: Trilokaal would like to see more focus on densification within the centers for housing construction. This way, we protect our rural area and preserve Teylingen's village character.

124455.explanation: Disagree, not 'always'. Opening a shop or a campsite is only allowed under conditions. For example, it must not come at the expense of the surroundings and possibly existing shops nearby.

124458.explanation: Not currently applicable. Only under conditions (small-scale and no reception of vulnerable newcomers) if the national government calls upon us due to the dispersion law. Then we are obligated to comply.

124463.explanation: Not a 'must' but a 'can'. This is not a government responsibility.

124468.explanation: Do give discounts to people who have to live on minimum income; that is already current policy.

124470.explanation: Only when necessary; the municipality is obligated to do so.

124475.explanation: And then truly enforce, not just drive around; enforcement is currently lacking. A zero-tolerance policy is needed.

124476.explanation: For example, at Voorhout station and other places where there is nuisance.

124482.explanation: Is existing policy. Of course, only if possible and people are capable of it.

124483.explanation: The municipality should actually have more scope to waive municipal taxes. Currently, this can only be done for people on social assistance benefits and not for working people with income at social assistance level because a higher asset limit applies to them.

124484.explanation: Only if necessary. In 2020, the group eligible for poverty assistance in Teylingen was expanded from 110% of the social minimum to households with income up to 120% of the social minimum. If poverty rises, an expansion to 130% may be considered.

124486.explanation: Must not come at the expense of parking spaces for hybrid and petrol cars. Charging points are possible at one’s own house/apartment.

124488.explanation: There is such a shortage in healthcare that you should be glad if there are enough hands at the bedside.

124489.explanation: Quality is more important than price. Care providers who undercut prices often don’t deliver. Cheap is expensive in the end.

124490.explanation: That would be nice, but it won’t work because the municipality does not have the financial capacity and this must not come at the expense of the level of facilities.

124495.explanation: Incomprehensible to people who have been waiting a long time for housing.

124498.explanation: Important in our water-rich environment that children learn to swim, including children from low-income families.

124501.explanation: The villages must retain their village character. This means no large-scale high-rise buildings.

124503.explanation: The number of parking spaces must meet the parking balance. Especially in the center of Voorhout, parking is a bottleneck for residents in surrounding streets.

124504.explanation: The church square should be car-free, and the rest of the shopping center low-traffic, so that a pleasant and attractive stay environment is created.

124505.explanation: The Teydelijk project is a good example of temporary housing for urgent seekers. It should be examined whether temporary homes for urgent seekers can be realized in more places.

124506.explanation: A district heating network does not fit in rural areas where houses are far apart. It is very costly and unsustainable due to unnecessary heat loss.

124507.explanation: Absolutely disagree. It is unwise and not future-proof due to extremely unsafe traffic location and close proximity to houseboats and residences. Causes health damage and noise pollution. Better to look for an alternative location on a business park in the region.

124509.explanation: Allowing housing on bulb fields means the end of the Bollenstreek.

124455.explanation: Small-scale sales at farms are possible and can even contribute to social connection. Large activities that do not fit the zoning can only proceed after assessment against rules and legislation, to prevent uncontrolled growth and deterioration of the countryside.

124456.explanation: In Teylingen, we encourage sustainable energy generation via solar panels, collective projects such as solar parks, and the Energy Cooperative Sun on Teylingen. The ChristenUnie supports sharing energy with people who are less well-off. This way, we help residents make sustainable changes together.

124458.explanation: ChristenUnie Teylingen sees asylum seeker reception as a shared responsibility. An asylum center (azc) request must be carefully weighed based on capacity, location, and integration. The municipality must at least meet its legal task for reception.

124463.explanation: ChristenUnie Teylingen does not consider a fireworks show on New Year’s Eve absolutely necessary. A joint party or other safe community celebration can be a good alternative.

124468.explanation: ChristenUnie Teylingen is in favor. The municipality already supports via the Youth Fund for Sports & Culture. Sport is important for health and social connection. We can explore ways to expand these opportunities.

124470.explanation: Good and safe school buildings are essential for a healthy learning environment and equal opportunities. Schools are part of the Integral Housing Plan (IHP), ensuring all schools are addressed.

124475.explanation: BOAs (special enforcement officers) provide visibility, but real safety requires cooperation: police, neighborhood officers, municipality, and aid agencies. ChristenUnie Teylingen also focuses on prevention and neighborhood initiatives for a safe society.

124476.explanation: More camera surveillance may seem to offer safety but infringes on privacy and creates an illusion of safety. ChristenUnie Teylingen chooses real solutions that strengthen safety and trust.

124482.explanation: Agree, within the person's capabilities. Socially meaningful work can contribute to participation and self-esteem, but always with attention to health and personal circumstances.

124483.explanation: Waiving municipal taxes helps residents with low incomes to make ends meet. Stopping this would increase poverty and debts, while basic security should be our priority.

124484.explanation: The current income threshold for poverty assistance is adequate and keeps support affordable and targeted for those who really need it.

124486.explanation: More charging points for electric cars are needed. At the same time, the STOMP principle remains important: prioritize walking, cycling, and public transport for sustainable mobility.

124487.explanation: In neighborhoods, 30 km/h is already the standard; on main through roads, 50 km/h remains. However, it should be examined whether Oosthoutlaan can be reduced to 30 km/h, as it has become a busy through road.

124488.explanation: Freedom in care is crucial: choosing a care provider that matches one’s faith, culture, or conviction is a must. This builds trust and strengthens self-direction.

124489.explanation: Invest in quality in youth care! It’s not just about the price, but good and effective help for children. Choice of care provider that fits faith, culture, or conviction. This way, we build a strong foundation for their future.

124490.explanation: The property tax (ozb) forms the basis for facilities such as roads, playgrounds, and maintenance. Without this income, general facilities are at risk. Moreover, the ozb in Teylingen is still below the regional average.

124493.explanation: No, first reduce vacancy and optimally use existing business parks. This prevents unnecessary expansion and preserves space for nature and housing. Only if that is insufficient, consider new sites.

124495.explanation: Allocate homes alternately. ChristenUnie Teylingen wants a housing policy that respects local needs and our social responsibility, with care for vulnerable groups such as status holders and people in crisis situations.

124498.explanation: Swimming lessons take a lot of school time due to transport and changing clothes. The question is whether schools even want this. The municipality is responsible for school housing, not the curriculum.

124499.explanation: No, ChristenUnie Teylingen opposes the establishment of a coffee shop. We do not want normalization of drug use and focus on a healthy and safe living environment for everyone. Prevention and assistance are our priorities.

124501.explanation: No, no tall residential flats. Maximum 5 floors and taking parking policy into account. More may be allowed in neighborhood redevelopment, but always with attention to livability and surroundings.

124503.explanation: No, no additional parking spaces are needed. The current parking policy is good and even generous. Extra spaces would unnecessarily consume space and harm livability and greenery.

124504.explanation: Yes, make the church square as car-free as possible and have cars as guests where possible. The new center plans have just been adopted; the final elaboration will follow in coming years, focusing on accessibility, livability, and an attractive center.

124505.explanation: Yes, more places for temporary housing are needed, but first explore solutions such as shared housing and better use of existing housing stock. Only then add new locations for temporary homes.

124506.explanation: Yes, a regional heat network helps us become independent of geopolitical influences. However, be critical of costs for residents and impacts on soil and nature, so sustainability does not come at the expense of livability.

124507.explanation: Renovation of the waste processing site is necessary to meet modern standards. But expansion at this location is undesirable due to infrastructure and living environment impact. Renewing can happen, but not expanding.

124508.explanation: Yes, continue cooperation with Lisse and Hillegom. Handle regional policy matters such as ISD and waste policy jointly, while maintaining each council’s own identity and wishes. Joint orientation sessions improve coordination and strong regional cooperation.

124509.explanation: Yes, oppose housing on bulb fields. Focus first on infill, shared housing, and building on or topping existing sites. This preserves the unique landscape and efficiently uses space before sacrificing valuable bulb fields.

124455.explanation: Farmers are under great pressure. By allowing space for secondary activities, we keep the rural area livable, economically healthy, and in the hands of local families.

124456.explanation: Sustainability should never become coercive. Initiatives must be voluntary, without burdening others with higher costs. This is not a task for the municipality.

124458.explanation: An asylum seekers center in Teylingen is excluded. Our villages are not reception locations for failing national policies. The housing shortage, pressure on facilities, and safety risks are too great. Teylingen must be for its own residents.

124463.explanation: Setting off fireworks belongs to New Year's Eve. Residents must continue to be able to do this themselves. A municipal show must not become a disguised alternative to replace personal fireworks. Freedom and tradition come first.

124468.explanation: Sports are important, but support must be careful and purposeful, not an automatic municipal obligation.

124470.explanation: Good and safe school buildings are a basic requirement for education. This is no luxury, but a core task.

124475.explanation: Safety is about tackling real nuisance, not about extra control over minor rules.

124476.explanation: More camera surveillance infringes privacy and does not solve structural safety problems. Safety requires visible presence, social cohesion, and targeted enforcement, not permanent monitoring of residents.

124482.explanation: Those who can contribute should do so. That strengthens self-reliance and reciprocity.

124483.explanation: Those who truly cannot pay must receive help. At the same time, the system must not become a free pass for structural dependency. Waiving charges requires tailored approach and control, so support reaches those in need without burdening taxpayers.

124484.explanation: Support must reach those who really need it. Broadening without focus makes assistance unsustainable.

124486.explanation: Parking spaces are scarce. Policy must be neutral and not favor specific groups.

124487.explanation: A general 30 km/h limit is symbolic policy. Traffic safety requires tailoring per street, not ideological speed reductions that unnecessarily affect accessibility, flow, and livability.

124488.explanation: In the Netherlands, care is accessible and equal for everyone. Those who have specific additional wishes or demands must arrange and finance these themselves. The municipality should not create or pay for exceptions to personal preferences.

124489.explanation: Price should never outweigh quality. Poor care ultimately costs more.

124490.explanation: Residents and entrepreneurs are already under pressure. The municipality must first save on itself.

124493.explanation: Employment is important, but must fit the scale and character of Teylingen.

124495.explanation: The housing shortage is urgent. It is unacceptable that our own residents wait for years while people from abroad get priority. Social rental homes are meant for the community that built Teylingen, not as a reception tool for failing migration policy.

124498.explanation: Swimming education is important but not a core task of the municipality. Parents and schools are primarily responsible. The municipality should focus on its legal tasks and prevent taking on more and more educational tasks.

124499.explanation: Teylingen currently has no coffee shop. If a regulated and strictly controlled coffee shop is established, it could help combat street dealing and crime. Supervision and regulation are more effective than an illegal market out of sight.

124501.explanation: High-rise buildings undermine the village scale and livability. Sassenheim, Voorhout, and Warmond are not cities.

124503.explanation: The car remains indispensable for many residents, families, and entrepreneurs. Sufficient parking spaces are essential for accessibility, livability, and the local economy.

124504.explanation: Entrepreneurs and residents depend on accessibility by car.

124505.explanation: Temporary housing does not solve the housing shortage but postpones the problem. It comes at the expense of quality and livability. Teylingen must focus on structural housing construction that suits our villages.

124506.explanation: Large-scale heating networks limit freedom of choice, can create unsafe situations, and involve enormous financial risks.

124507.explanation: Building a new waste processing plant at this location threatens the safety and livability of nearby residents. The VAB site must be moved to a more suitable location, away from residential areas.

124508.explanation: The HLT cooperation has led to more bureaucracy, higher costs, and less control for Teylingen. Decisions are made further away from our residents. Teylingen must take control again and choose direct democratic oversight instead of administrative erosion.

124509.explanation: Bulb fields define our economy, culture, and identity. If they disappear, Teylingen as we know it disappears. We cherish our beautiful living environment.

124455.explanation: A small-scale shop and additional tourist activities are fine.

124456.explanation: This is an activity initiated by the residents themselves.

124458.explanation: Spreading does not solve the problem; it spreads the problems.

124468.explanation: Introduce a different scheme: if you receive social assistance and want to join a sports club but cannot afford it, enter a program where you do volunteer work in compensation for the hours you spend on sports.

124470.explanation: But only in moderation and what is truly necessary.

124475.explanation: With one condition: the enforcement officers must be much more visible on the streets! And not just to issue fines, but especially to tackle abuses. Aggressive behavior, littering, undermining, to name a few examples.

124476.explanation: Cameras create a false sense of security. People must continue to address each other’s behavior. And report if something strange happens. After which the police/enforcement officers must take immediate action.

124483.explanation: Introduce a new scheme: if you cannot pay municipal taxes with money, you compensate by doing volunteer work. Each hour of volunteer work reduces your municipal tax bill.

124484.explanation: Currently, many people just above the social assistance threshold fall through the cracks everywhere.

124486.explanation: The Party for Teylingen made sure that people are allowed to use flat charging cables to charge their electric cars from home. All those charging parking spaces come at the expense of parking availability for other cars.

124487.explanation: Not on main roads; it should remain 50 km per hour there.

124488.explanation: You must be satisfied with the care you receive.

124489.explanation: With attention to quality, of course.

124490.explanation: All other political parties voted in favor of increasing the property tax to 63%. The Party for Teylingen was the only one to vote against any tax increase!

124493.explanation: But in moderation. And existing business parks can be improved and beautified.

124495.explanation: Absolutely not! Based on the constitution, everyone must be equal. So refugees with a residence permit must follow the same procedure as housing seekers who were born here.

124498.explanation: We live in a water-rich area. Prevention is better than cure.

124499.explanation: The Party for Teylingen helped prevent the opening of a coffee shop.

124501.explanation: Higher residential flats are allowed in Teylingen. With additional floors, we can create more homes without harming the nature in the rural area.

124503.explanation: The municipality and province want fewer parking spaces. The Party for Teylingen believes there must be enough parking for our residents!

124504.explanation: Make the Hoofdstraat in the center a pedestrian promenade! The Party for Teylingen has advocated this for years.

124505.explanation: With priority for our own residents, the Party for Teylingen believes.

124506.explanation: Geothermal energy from the ground under Teylingen/Noordwijk is worth investigating.

124507.explanation: Alternative locations must first be considered where this waste processing plant can also be built.

124508.explanation: We pay 50% of the costs but have only 33% control; Teylingen must take more charge in this cooperation.

124509.explanation: We must preserve the 2,625 hectares of prime bulb fields!

statements.title: A regional district heating network should be established for the Bollenstreek.

titleParts.glossary: Network of underground pipes that brings hot water from a central source to homes and buildings, for both heating and tap water.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The construction of a renewed waste processing plant on 's Gravendamseweg in Voorhout should proceed.
In Teylingen there should be more municipal enforcement officers (BOA’s) who deal with safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: Tall apartment blocks should be allowed in Teylingen.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Teylingen should continue its cooperation with the municipalities of Lisse and Hillegom.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: In Teylingen there should be more CCTV surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: There should be more places for temporary housing.
There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: Villages or small towns that are part of the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to provide funding for school swimming lessons.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar blijven stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: More business parks should be built in Teylingen.
The center of Sassenheim should be car-free.
The municipality must prevent housing development on bulb-growing land.
